mito.walk8_down.styleNotes:
hd_walk8_256: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER WALK CYCLE sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer, face visible). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions of the attached reference character EXACTLY: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build with a solid chest, thick limbs and a broad stable stance - NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference. Every frame keeps EXACTLY the same head size, the same total height and the same sturdy body width. The sword stays sheathed at his hip in every frame (never in hand). 8-frame WALK CYCLE with proper alternating footsteps: frame1 = CONTACT A (his LEFT foot planted forward, right foot back, arms counter-swing), frame2 = weight drop (body 2-3px lower), frame3 = PASSING (feet together under the body, body at mid height), frame4 = push-off (body 2-3px higher), frame5 = CONTACT B (his RIGHT foot planted forward, left foot back - the MIRROR step of frame1), frame6 = weight drop, frame7 = PASSING (feet together), frame8 = push-off. The forward foot MUST alternate: left foot leads in frame1, right foot leads in frame5. Arms swing opposite to the legs each step. Both feet clearly visible and readable against the ground. The haori hem and the vermilion cord tassels sway naturally with each step. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell, head at the same height in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.

mito.idle8_down.styleNotes:
hd_motion8_256: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER IDLE (breathing) sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer, face visible). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same slim-but-sturdy balance, same total height. NOT wider, NOT stockier than the reference, EXACTLY the same head size, total height and body width in every frame. Standing relaxed and natural, feet CLOSE together only about shoulder-width apart, legs nearly vertical - NOT a wide horse stance, NOT braced for combat - and the feet NEVER move, the sword sheathed at his hip. 8-frame subtle BREATHING LOOP: frame1 = neutral stance, frame2 = slow inhale, chest and shoulders rise 1-2px, frame3 = inhale peak, head lifts 1px, frame4 = breath held, cord tassels drift, frame5 = exhale begins, shoulders settle, frame6 = lowest relaxed point, body 1-2px lower than neutral, frame7 = calm blink (eyes closed) while the body returns toward neutral, frame8 = eyes open, back to the neutral stance so the loop repeats seamlessly. The haori hem, the vermilion cord tassels and the small paper streamers on the sword sway very subtly throughout. The silhouette change between frames is SMALL and smooth - this is a quiet idle, not an action. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.

mito.atk8_down.styleNotes:
hd_motion8_256: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER SWORD ATTACK sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. He draws and swings his short ritual sword: a straight bright blade with a WHITE hilt wrapped with a thin sacred rope and small white paper streamers that flutter with the motion. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same slim-but-sturdy balance, same total height. NOT wider, NOT stockier than the reference, EXACTLY the same head size and body size in every frame; the character body is drawn at about 70 percent of the cell height so the fully extended sword always fits INSIDE the cell with margin. 8-frame VERTICAL OVERHEAD CUT staying FRONT-FACING: his face, chest and hips keep facing the viewer squarely in EVERY frame - he never turns sideways, never swings horizontally. frame1 = WINDUP start, both hands grip the hilt in front of his chest, frame2 = WINDUP peak, the blade raised STRAIGHT UP above his head, vertical, frame3 = STRIKE, the blade chopping straight DOWN in front of his torso, frame4 = STRIKE end, arms fully extended downward-forward, the blade tip pointing at the ground in front of his feet - the decisive key pose, frame5 = FOLLOW-THROUGH, the blade low in front of him, shoulders dropped, frame6 = FOLLOW-THROUGH settles, frame7 = RECOVERY, he lifts the blade back up toward a centered two-hand guard, frame8 = centered front-facing guard stance (loops cleanly into frame1). NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect, NO glow: draw ONLY the solid body and the solid sword in every frame. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.

Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.

bg.satoyama_sky.styleNotes:
battle_background_320_180: Wide pixel-art parallax BACKGROUND for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG field: a luminous clear morning sky over a peaceful satoyama countryside horizon. Soft cumulus clouds, gentle distant green hills with terraced rice paddies, faint atmospheric haze near the horizon line. Bright, open, hopeful mood with rich saturated colors - spring sunlight. The horizon line sits in the lower third; the upper two thirds are sky and clouds. NO characters, NO buildings in the foreground, no text. Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art, fine readable pixel clusters, painterly SNES-era sky gradients.

mito.iai8_left.styleNotes:
hd_motion8_256: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER IAI (battou-jutsu) sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing LEFT (full clean side profile; the white scabbard faces the viewer). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. PROPORTION: match the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same body width, same slim-but-sturdy balance, same total height. The character body is drawn at about 70 percent of the cell height so the fully extended sword always fits INSIDE the cell with margin. 8-frame IAI DRAW-CUT, one continuous motion: frame1 = IAI STANCE: knees slightly bent, body lowered and coiled, LEFT hand gripping the white scabbard at his hip, RIGHT hand resting on the hilt, the blade STILL SHEATHED, eyes focused, frame2 = deeper crouch, maximum tension, still sheathed (frames 1-2 are the charge loop), frame3 = THE DRAW BEGINS: the blade halfway out of the scabbard, body starting to twist, frame4 = BATTOU: the blade fully drawn in one lightning horizontal draw-cut toward the LEFT, arm fully extended, blade level at chest height, body lunging slightly forward - the decisive key pose, frame5 = FOLLOW-THROUGH: the swing decelerates past his body, paper streamers on the hilt trailing, frame6 = settling, weight forward, blade low, frame7 = NOTO: he guides the blade back toward the scabbard mouth, frame8 = sheathed again, calm stance (loops cleanly to frame1). NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect: draw ONLY the solid body, scabbard and sword. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
